title: Temporary vs. Permanent Accounts: Which of the following accounts...
summary: Distinguish between temporary and permanent accounts in the accounting cycle.
prompt: Which of the following accounts is a temporary account?

options.text: Cannot be determined from the given information
Temporary accounts are accounts that accumulate balances over a single

solution: Temporary accounts are accounts that accumulate balances over a single accounting period and are closed at the end of the period to the income summary account. Sales revenue is a classic example of a temporary account, as it resets to zero at the end of the year. Understanding the distinction between temporary and permanent accounts is crucial for proper accounting practices and accurate financial reporting.
